text.title: Cost Estimation Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding Cost Estimation. Each pairs a binary check and a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Cost estimate MUST decompose to WBS leaves with named success criteria. Estimating at higher levels is forbidden for projects ≥25k.
rule.rationale: Top-down estimates hide variance and resist defence. Bottom-up forces discovery of hidden work.

rule.statement: Each WBS leaf MUST carry three-point estimates (optimistic, most-likely, pessimistic) feeding a PERT mean.
rule.rationale: Single-point estimates produce false precision; PERT exposes the variance the project actually carries.

rule.statement: Contingency reserve MUST be sized from the risk register, not as a flat 10/15/20% padding. Each contingency line ties to a named risk.
rule.rationale: Flat contingency is unhedged; risk-driven contingency is defensible to buyer scrutiny.

rule.statement: Management reserve (for unknown-unknowns) MUST be tracked SEPARATELY from contingency. It is approved by sponsor, not consumed by PM.
rule.rationale: Comingling reserves lets PMs spend management reserve on planned risks, leaving no buffer for genuine unknowns.

rule.statement: Each three-point estimate MUST cite ≥1 historical reference (similar engagement, leaf, or industry benchmark).
rule.rationale: Uncited estimates are vibes. Citations make calibration possible and resist optimism bias.

rule.statement: If preconditions for Cost Estimation fail (see Skip-If list in AGENTS.md), the operator MUST skip this methodology and document the fallback reason instead of fabricating an output.
rule.rationale: Applying Cost Estimation outside its envelope produces false-precision artefacts. Explicit skip preserves epistemic discipline.
